text: Wuyang is capable of keeping up with most other heroes, quickly repositioning and taking high ground with his   Rushing Torrent . He has great poke damage, being able to direct his orbs while safely hidden behind cover and hitting enemies behind obstacles such as barriers. This lets him pressure the enemy team and generate ult charge with low risk. He is also one of only a few support heroes who can actively heal an ally through enemy barriers. His ultimate,   Tidal Blast , is effective both defensively and offensively. It provides overhealth, a burst of healing, movement speed and it will damage and knockdown enemies who are nearby when it expires. It can save endangered allies, zone enemies from an area or be mixed with an ally’s ultimate for a deadly combo. While he is capable of healing a substantial amount, especially when combined with the direct healing and the healing amplification of his   Guardian Wave , his healing is tied to a resource meter which regenerates slowly, so he prefers fights to end quickly. He will struggle vs area-of-effect damage and heavy burst since he has no means of preventing damage. Another weakness is highly mobile heroes, such as Genji and Tracer . Since Wuyang only has Rushing Torrent for movement, enemies who can keep up are a problem. They can also close in quickly. This denies him poke damage which can turn the duel in his favor and by getting close enough, Wuyang won’t be able to charge his orbs and will deal significantly reduced damage. General and   Xuanwu Staff Don't be afraid to take high ground positions. Wuyang has no fall-off on his weapon and   Guardian Wave will continue off ledges along the ground below. In between fights, if you don’t already have ult, try to poke the enemy team for some extra ult charge. Watch your ammo and make sure to reload before the team fight. Your primary fire is a great way to follow up engagements by your team's flanker and dive heroes. You can make great use of pings to pinpoint enemies behind cover. Other great indicators are audio (e.g footsteps, voice lines), bullet/ability trajectory and tracers. You can 'feel out' the position of enemies by charging orbs and releasing control in the general direction of where you suspect a player is at. If it hits, you can switch to guiding orbs to that area.   Restorative Stream Also called Water Ring, this is both Wuyang's passive and active healing. Be deliberate and avoid 'feathering' with Wuyang's healing. It takes 2 seconds to begin regenerating healing resource and if you do more than single press to swap the passive healing, you will restart the timer and generate little or no resource. Wuyang can be out of line-of-sight with the ally he is actively healing for up to 1 second. You can use this delay to weave in and out of cover, but make sure to vary your timing and position as much as possible so that you aren't easy to predict. Where possible, let your fellow support heal up allies who are not actively taking damage. For example, between fights. This ensures you have full healing resources when the team fight begins. The Water Ring indicator is a great way to keep track of an ally, such as a Genji you want to combo your ultimate with.   Rushing Torrent Rushing Torrent is Wuyang's only movement skill. It enhances his Movement speed and jump height , allowing him to keep up with allies, quickly reposition, take high ground or evade enemies. If you're using Rushing Torrent but don't need it, cancel it to start the cooldown. Wuyang receives a burst of speed when activating Rushing Torrent, which can often be enough on its own to reposition. Familiarize yourself with the maps and what positions you can reach and gaps you can cross with Rushing Torrent and which areas will require you to use nearby obstacles as steps. Be mindful when jumping during Rushing Torrent. Not only does jumping make you easier to hit, but improperly timed jumps can make it so that you don't reach a desired position before the duration runs out. Wuyang cannot crouch while using Rushing Torrent, which limits your ability to dodge. Try to use the enhanced mobility to get behind cover quickly instead, especially if you are caught in the open. On maps with a speed boost , save Rushing Torrent until the boost is over as the boost is already at the movement speed cap.   Guardian Wave Guardian Wave is a versatile ability that heals and amplifies healing received by all allies hit (including Wuyang), as well as damaging and knocking back any enemies that are hit. It is Wuyang's only method of healing himself. While it can do decent damage up close, it is best to think of Guardian Wave as a supporting tool before anything else. A well timed application of the healing amplification can be the difference in a team fight. If   Rushing Torrent is on cooldown, try to keep Guardian Wave available in case you need to peel for yourself. Activating Guardian Wave restores some of your healing resources, so try and use up some beforehand to maximize the benefit. Guardian Wave isn't particularly tall and it can easily be dodged by accident. Try to read your allies' movement and keep track of their various movement skills. While the knock back portion of the wave isn't very strong compared to most knock back effects, it does add some utility to the skill. The movement pattern the enemy is forced into when struck with the wave is predictable, making a follow up shot much easier to land. It can cancel out the momentum of other, non-channeled movement abilities. For example, Winston 's   Jump Pack . Pushing him back as he lands can put him out of the primary-fire range of his   Tesla Cannon . The displacement can be the difference between life and death for allies caught by abilities like Roadhog 's   Chain Hook or Junker Queen 's   Jagged Blade . It can be used to push enemies off of ledges. While environmental kills are possibly, a more common use case is knocking enemies off of highground. For example, Reaper after he uses   Shadow Step .   Tidal Blast Wuyang's ultimate ability, which is good both offensively and defensively. It can be cast on any ally or Wuyang himself. The target receives a large amount of overhealth , a movement speed bonus and will explode in an area centered around them. The explosion knocks down and deals damage to enemies that are hit. Once the explosion happens, the target is also healed. Tidal Blast takes longer to cast when used on yourself, so avoid using it while you're taking damage -- doubly so when you are also low on health. The knockdown component of Tidal Blast will cancel Channeled abilities (   Block ,   Focusing Beam ) and ultimates (   Death Blossom ,   Coalescence .) While it won't cancel non-channeled ultimates, Tidal Blast is a good deterrent against some ultimates, such as   Dragonblade . Using Tidal Blast after an enemy Wuyang uses his can be an effective counter, especially when the targets are both tanks. The Overhealth will help protect them and the knockdown will trigger even while your ally is knocked down from the enemy Tidal Blast. Tidal Blast synergies: Most tanks work well, though Sigma and Roadhog may struggle to close the distance even with the movement speed bonus if the enemy team is particularly mobile. Dive , close-range Damage heroes ( Genji , Mei , Tracer , Venture , Reaper ) and some Supports ( Ana , Brigitte , Lúcio , Moira ) also work well. Ultimates:   Annihilation ,   Cage Fight ,   Death Blossom ,   Dragonblade ,   Duplicate ,   EMP ,   Terra Surge .
